address: 79 Baldwin St (Golden Grams Culture Boutique / Golden Gate Ltd.)
note: Close-up of a bailiff termination warrant notice posted in the shop window. Document from Bailiff Solutions Inc. addressed to Golden Grams Ltd. d/b/a Culture Boutique at 79 Baldwin St, Toronto. Landlord: Mitcham Ent. Ltd. Amount owing: ~$84,575.41. Green artificial grass border visible around the document. Interior shows wooden ceiling beams. Not useful for full facade reconstruction but confirms business identity and address.
condition_notes: Close-up of legal notice only. Cannot assess building condition from this shot.

note: Second close-up of the same bailiff termination warrant. Slightly wider angle showing more of the green artificial turf border and the blue document. Same information as photo 1. Not useful for facade reconstruction.
condition_notes: Close-up of legal notice only. Duplicate angle.

note: Third close-up of the bailiff notice, slightly different angle. Same document content. Not useful for facade reconstruction.
condition_notes: Close-up of legal notice only. Duplicate angle.

note: Wide shot showing the full storefront of 79 Baldwin St at dusk. The building is a 1-storey commercial unit with a modern dark-clad facade. The storefront has full-width glazing with the 'Golden Grams Culture Boutique' sign on a white rectangular panel at left. Interior visible through glass showing exposed wood beam ceiling, white walls, and retail display cases. The address number 77 is visible at far left suggesting this may span 77-79.

windows_detail.note: Two large storefront plate glass panels, full-height, dark metal frames

doors.note: Glass door set within the storefront glazing system, dark frame

condition_notes: Modern renovation with clean dark metal/composite cladding over original facade. Storefront in good condition. Dusk lighting. Business appears closed/terminated based on bailiff notice in other photos.

note: Wide shot showing three adjacent buildings. Left: 3-storey red brick building with graffiti-covered dark ground floor and green diamond-patterned facade (labeled 'Huey' at bottom). Centre: Nomnom Fruit shop with bright red painted storefront surround, second floor brick with two white-framed windows. Right edge: another building. The left building has a green sage-coloured ground floor with diamond lattice wood trim and a dark awning. The main Nomnom Fruit building is 2 storeys with a prominent red storefront.

windows_detail.note: Full storefront glazing with red surround, menu photos displayed in window
Two white-framed rectangular windows on brick upper storey, evenly spaced

doors.note: Red-framed commercial entrance at right side of storefront

condition_notes: Upper brick in reasonable condition. Storefront heavily modified with bright red paint over original. Satellite dish on second floor wall. Utility conduit visible. Adjacent left building is heavily graffitied at ground level.

note: 2-storey red brick commercial building. Ground floor is a tattoo parlour with extensive flash art sheets displayed in the window and a wooden plank sign reading 'Inky Dinky Tattoo & Piercing - Walk-ins Welcome!'. Upper floor is red running-bond brick with 2 white-framed windows. A weathered wood plank sign/awning hangs above the storefront. Black painted base/bulkhead below storefront.

windows_detail.note: Two large storefront windows filled with tattoo flash art displays, white frames
Two white-framed rectangular windows on upper brick floor, evenly spaced

doors.note: Recessed entrance between storefront windows with teal/turquoise curtain

condition_notes: Upper brick in fair condition with some discolouration and staining. Rustic wood plank awning sign weathered. Black painted bulkhead. Storefront windows covered with tattoo flash. Multiple signs and banners. LED signs in upper portion above second floor (purple illuminated advertising).

note: 2-storey building with a stucco/painted upper floor in a warm ochre/tan colour. Ground floor has multiple commercial units with heavy signage: a vape shop at left with pink/magenta advertising, 'Latin Taste' restaurant in centre-right with red and white signage and food photos, and a colourful mural/artwork to the right. The upper floor has one large 4-pane window (dark frames, possibly casement or fixed). A red scalloped decorative trim band runs along the storefront top. The facade material appears to be rendered/stucco over masonry. Small decorative medallion/relief on upper wall.

windows_detail.note: Multiple storefront windows for vape shop and Latin Taste restaurant, extensive signage covering glass
One large multi-pane window (2x2 grid) with dark frames on tan stucco wall

condition_notes: Upper stucco in fair condition with some staining and a small decorative medallion/relief. Ground floor heavily altered with multiple commercial tenants and overlapping signage. Colourful mural on right side. Red scalloped trim band is a distinctive ornamental feature.

note: 2-storey commercial building. Ground floor is Coral Sea Fish shop with a prominent bright blue painted metal/steel frame surround, large plate glass windows (one with security grille partially visible), and the shop name in flowing white script on blue signage above. Upper floor is not clearly visible (partially obscured) but adjacent buildings show brick. The storefront projects slightly with blue-painted steel framing. Colourful mosaic tile or painted mural on sidewalk/base in front. The building appears to be a narrow commercial unit in a row.

windows_detail.note: Two large storefront windows, one with security accordion grille, blue steel frames
Upper floor partially obscured by angle; appears to have window(s) on brick wall

doors.note: Blue-framed glass door between the two storefront windows, small step up

condition_notes: Bright blue steel storefront surround in decent shape. Security grille on one window. Adjacent party wall on right is exposed concrete/stucco with graffiti. Colourful mosaic/painted produce displays at sidewalk level. Upper facade partially obscured.

note: 2-storey building visible from an oblique angle. The upper floor shows light blue painted brick/stucco with graffiti (large black bubble-letter tags). Ground floor has dark brown corrugated metal/ribbed cladding forming storefront with security grilles and dark-framed glazing. Yellow-outlined black graffiti lettering on the corrugated panels. A small exposed brick section is visible at the roofline with a chimney/vent pipe. The building appears to share a party wall with 194 Baldwin to the right (a gable-roofed building).

windows_detail.note: Two large panels with corrugated metal surrounds, partially covered by security film/grilles and graffiti
No visible windows on upper level from this angle; wall covered in light blue paint and graffiti

doors.note: Dark-framed entry within corrugated cladding storefront

condition_notes: Heavily graffitied on both floors. Upper wall painted light blue with large black bubble-letter tags. Ground floor corrugated cladding covered in large yellow-outlined graffiti. Security grilles on storefront windows. Chimney/vent pipe visible at roofline. Overall neglected appearance.

note: 2.5-storey bay-and-gable building, one of the most architecturally significant on this stretch. Red brick facade with a steep front-facing gable. The gable peak has dark-painted bargeboard trim. Upper brick wall heavily covered in graffiti (black and white tags, 'KOSAK' visible). Ground floor has a green awning/sign with 'TOM'S PLACE' text partially visible at the right edge. A corrugated metal storefront with security grilles and graffiti dominates the ground level. The roofline shows the classic Victorian gable form with finials/posts at the gable ends. Red brick is visible between the graffiti on the second floor.

windows_detail.note: Two storefront windows behind corrugated metal/security grilles, heavily graffitied
At least one window visible behind graffiti, appears to have segmental arch head in brick
Small window in gable peak, partially visible behind graffiti/tags

doors.note: Entrance under green Tom's Place awning/signage at right side

condition_notes: Extremely heavy graffiti covering most of the upper facade brickwork. Ground floor corrugated cladding also heavily tagged. Classic Victorian bay-and-gable form still readable beneath the vandalism. Bargeboard and gable profile intact. Brick appears structurally sound but aesthetically compromised. Finial posts at gable ends still present.

note: Additional angle of the same bay-and-gable building at 194, but slightly shifted to show 192 more clearly. This appears to be the same gable-roofed structure or its immediate neighbour. The view shows the steep gable with dark bargeboard, red brick upper facade covered in graffiti ('KOSAK' tag prominent), and the ground floor with corrugated metal panels and green awning. The green 'Tom's Place' sign is more visible in this angle. Very similar to 194 - possibly a paired semi-detached bay-and-gable building sharing the gable form.

windows_detail.note: Storefront windows behind corrugated metal panels and grilles
Window partially visible behind heavy graffiti
Small gable window under the peak

doors.note: Entrance within corrugated storefront, under green sign

condition_notes: Same heavy graffiti coverage as 194. 'KOSAK' tag very prominent across upper brick. Gable form intact. Corrugated metal ground floor with security grilles. Structurally appears sound but extremely vandalized.

note: Clearer view showing the Tom's Place signage. Green sign with gold/yellow lettering reads 'Tom's Place' with subtitle 'Celebrating 50 Years in Business'. The sign is mounted on a green painted band above the ground floor. Above, the 2.5-storey bay-and-gable brick building is visible with the steep gable, dark bargeboard, and heavily graffitied red brick. The relationship between 190-194 becomes clearer: Tom's Place occupies the ground floor of what appears to be one or more Victorian row houses. Black and white graffiti covers the upper brick.

windows_detail.note: Storefront fully covered by Tom's Place signage and corrugated metal/security panels
Window barely visible behind dense graffiti
Gable peak window

storefront.signage_text: Tom's Place - Celebrating 50 Years in Business

condition_notes: Upper facade almost entirely covered in graffiti. Tom's Place green signage is prominent and maintained. Victorian gable form readable. Gold lettering on green sign is distinctive. Building is a Kensington Market landmark (50 years in business per signage).

note: Slightly wider angle showing more of the Tom's Place building and its context. The gable roof profile is clearly visible. Heavy graffiti on upper brick. Green Tom's Place sign visible. To the right, the adjacent building (188) with similar corrugated storefront cladding and graffiti is visible. Packages/boxes stacked on sidewalk. The Victorian gable with bargeboard is the key architectural feature.

windows_detail.note: Covered by Tom's Place storefront panels
Obscured by graffiti
Gable window

condition_notes: Duplicate angle of 190 Baldwin. Same heavy graffiti, same green Tom's Place sign. Packages on sidewalk.

note: 2-storey flat-roofed red brick building viewed from oblique angle. The upper floor has exposed red brick covered in extensive graffiti - large white bubble letters and a bold yellow-outlined block letter piece dominate. The second floor shows at least one window (barely visible through graffiti). Ground floor has dark corrugated metal storefront cladding with a green sign/awning at left (partial view of Tom's Place next door). A white horizontal band separates ground and upper floors. The building reads as a simple 2-storey Victorian commercial row building with a flat roof and parapet.

windows_detail.note: Single storefront window behind corrugated metal cladding, barely visible
Two windows barely visible behind dense graffiti on upper brick wall

doors.note: Commercial entrance within the corrugated storefront

condition_notes: Upper brick almost completely covered in graffiti (large white and yellow-outlined pieces). Ground floor corrugated metal cladding obscures original storefront. White horizontal band between floors may be a remnant string course or painted division. Structurally appears intact but severely vandalized.

note: Wider angle of 188 Baldwin showing more context. Same 2-storey flat-roofed brick building with heavy graffiti on upper floor (white bubble letters, large yellow-outlined block piece). Ground floor has dark corrugated panels. Adjacent to Tom's Place (190) on the left. On the right, additional buildings are visible. The roofline shows a simple flat parapet. The graffiti is extremely extensive, covering essentially the entire upper facade.

windows_detail.note: Storefront window behind corrugated cladding
Two windows behind graffiti

condition_notes: Same as primary 188 angle. Duplicate view confirms heavy graffiti coverage and corrugated ground floor. Colourful striped awning barely visible at far right edge (likely next building). Packages and waste visible at sidewalk level.

note: 1.5-storey building with a front-facing gable. The entire facade is painted white brick with prominent green/teal accent paint forming geometric rectangular outlines on the upper gable wall. Sign reads '6x8 Market' in dark text on the white brick above the storefront. Ground floor has a full-width storefront with green/teal painted frames - large plate glass windows and a central double door (one with a peace symbol). Address number '184' visible on the right pilaster. A striped retractable awning (white and grey stripes) spans the storefront top. Chain-link fence and a painted flower mural (red daisy with an eyeball) on the left party wall. The gable peak has dark brown/grey trim. To the right, the yellow facade of 182 (Cheese Magic) is visible.

windows_detail.note: Three large storefront windows with teal/green frames. Left window has an AC unit. Heart decorations and merchandise visible inside.
Two green/teal painted rectangular outlines on white brick gable - may be sealed former windows or purely decorative paint

doors.note: Central double glass doors with teal frames, peace symbol on one door panel

condition_notes: Recently painted white brick in clean condition. Teal/green storefront frames in good repair. Striped awning intact. Decorative painted green rectangles on gable. Red flower mural on left party wall (painted on neighbouring grey stucco). The facade is a cheerful, artful renovation. Small air conditioner in left window.

note: Very similar to the first 184 photo, slightly different framing. Same white-painted brick gable facade with teal/green accents. '6x8 Market' sign, striped awning, teal storefront frames, and peace-symbol door all visible. The gable peak trim (dark brown) is slightly more visible. The flower mural and chain-link fence on the left party wall are clearer. Confirms all details from the primary photo.

windows_detail.note: Three storefront windows with teal frames
Painted green rectangles on white gable

condition_notes: Duplicate angle of 184 Baldwin. Confirms clean white-painted brick and teal storefront in good condition.

note: 2.5-storey bay-and-gable building with a dramatically painted bright yellow facade. The gable is clad in white clapboard/vinyl siding with a large circular white decorative medallion/oculus feature in the gable peak (appears non-functional, decorative). Below the gable, the second floor has one window (white frame, likely 1-over-1) set in the yellow-painted surface. The ground floor is a vibrant cheese shop: 'Cheese Magic' in red cursive script on a bright yellow background. Display windows show cheese photos and menu items in red-framed panels. A red horizontal band divides storefront from upper wall. Yellow paint with white polka dots covers the upper portion. The building is a classic Kensington Market bay-and-gable type that has been painted in a whimsical, eye-catching scheme.

windows_detail.note: Two display windows with red frames showing cheese products and menu photos. A smaller window above the display shows yellow-framed panes.
Single white-framed window on bright yellow painted wall with white polka dots
Large circular white medallion/oculus in gable peak - appears decorative rather than glazed

doors.note: Yellow-framed entrance door within the storefront

condition_notes: Bold yellow paint job with white polka dots is distinctive and well-maintained. Red cursive 'Cheese Magic' lettering is a Kensington Market landmark. White clapboard gable with circular medallion in good condition. The building's Victorian bay-and-gable form is intact beneath the decorative paint scheme. Upper yellow paint extends to the gable springline where white clapboard takes over.

note: 2.5-storey bay-and-gable building adjacent to Cheese Magic (182). The gable is clad in white/light grey vinyl siding or clapboard. A circular white decorative medallion/oculus is visible in the gable peak, similar to 182. Below, the second floor window has a distinctive arched or hooded surround in white/grey trim. The ground floor is the 'coal and curry' restaurant with a black-painted storefront fascia bearing the name in white cursive script. Large plate glass windows with dark frames. A dark metal awning/hood projects above the storefront. To the left, the bright yellow Cheese Magic building is partially visible. The gable trim is grey/white with a simple profile.

windows_detail.note: Two large storefront windows with dark frames, restaurant interior visible
Single window with decorative white/grey arched hood or surround moulding on upper storey, very distinctive
Circular white medallion/oculus in gable peak, matching 182 next door

doors.note: Dark-framed glass restaurant entrance at right side of storefront

window_hood.note: Distinctive arched hood/surround on second-floor window

condition_notes: White/light grey siding on upper floors in good condition. Black-painted storefront fascia is clean and well-maintained. The arched window hood on the second floor is an attractive heritage detail. Circular oculus in gable matches the neighbouring 182. Victorian gable form well-preserved. Arabic script visible on green sign above storefront (possibly a secondary business or upper-floor tenant).

note: 2.5-storey bay-and-gable building. The gable is clad in white/light grey clapboard or vinyl siding, matching the neighbours at 180 and 182 - these three buildings form a Victorian gable row. A circular white decorative medallion/oculus is again visible in the gable peak. The second floor has one window with a similar arched/hooded surround as 180. The ground floor houses two businesses: 'One Heart' on the left (teal/mint green painted fascia with pink neon-style text) and 'Tacos 101' on the right (dark green frame with a large purple/maroon banner flag and yellow signage). The storefront is divided between the two tenants. Utility pole partially obscures the view. To the left, the Coal and Curry storefront (180) is visible.

windows_detail.note: Left side: One Heart with teal frame and display window. Right side: Tacos 101 with dark green frame and two windows. LED/neon signage visible.
Single window with decorative arched hood moulding, matching 180 next door
Circular medallion/oculus in gable peak, part of the row pattern with 180 and 182

doors.note: One Heart entrance, teal/mint painted frame
Tacos 101 entrance, dark green frame, 'OPEN' sign visible

condition_notes: Upper clapboard/siding in good condition. Two active commercial tenants at ground level. Teal/mint 'One Heart' fascia is bright and well-maintained. Dark green 'Tacos 101' frames with colourful banner flag. The arched window hood on second floor matches 180 Baldwin, confirming this is a matched row of three Victorian gable buildings (178-180-182). The gable oculus details are a distinctive common element. Red text along the base reads what appears to be a website or social media handle.
